description_long: This is a pair of metal scissors with two elongated, slightly curved blades joined at a central pivot. The handles are oval-shaped and appear to be made from the same material as the blades, with no visible padding or ergonomic features. The finger loops are symmetrical and large enough to accommodate multiple fingers, suggesting a design for general-purpose use. The blades are straight and taper to a rounded tip, prioritizing safety over precision. The surface of the scissors is matte gray, indicating a utilitarian finish rather than a decorative one. There are no visible brand markings or logos on any part of the object.
description: A matte gray pair of metal scissors with symmetrical oval handles and rounded blade tips, designed for general-purpose cutting.
description_view_0: A narrow side profile showing the thinness of the blades and handles, with the pivot point visible as a small circle.
description_view_1: A top-down view displaying both blades partially open and the symmetrical oval handles clearly, with the pivot at the center.
description_view_2: A reverse side profile similar to view 0, emphasizing the slim construction and the alignment of the blades and handles.
description_view_3: A bottom-up view mirroring view 1, showing the open blades and oval handles from the opposite side.
attribute_handle_type: Symmetrical oval handles, uncoated, not padded, ambidextrous, no special grip features.
attribute_blade_joint_type: Central visible rivet joint, not adjustable, standard fixed design.
attribute_finger_loop_design: Two equal-sized oval loops, smooth surface, no anatomical contour or soft inserts, accommodates multiple fingers.
attribute_blade_tip_style: Rounded tip, designed for safety and general-purpose cutting.
